review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the NIST AC-6(3) control, focusing on least privilege and network access restrictions. The RBAC functions are correctly named and map well to Kubernetes security controls. The network policy function is technically accurate but could benefit from a more descriptive name to clearly indicate the focus on network access to privileged commands.
naming_improvements: Rename 'k8s_networkpolicy_restrict_privileged_command_access' to 'k8s_networkpolicy_restrict_privileged_command_network_access' to better reflect the network aspect of the control.
title: Least Privilege - Least privilege | network access to privileged commands authorize network access
step1_reasoning: The control AC-6(3) focuses on ensuring least privilege for network access to privileged commands. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to RBAC configurations to enforce least privilege by ensuring roles and bindings do not grant excessive permissions, particularly avoiding cluster-admin bindings. Additionally, NetworkPolicies can be used to restrict network access to pods that execute privileged commands, ensuring only authorized network paths are allowed.
